nodes.label: AI as brainstorming partner for activity ideas
nodes.label_negative: AI as unable to generate innovative or tailored ideas

nodes.label: AI ability to tailor activities to mixed developmental levels
nodes.label_negative: AI struggle with balancing challenge for different ages
nodes.grounding_span: tailor activities for a variety of children in a group so all the activities are accessible

nodes.label: AI performance on physical vs problem-solving activities
nodes.label_negative: AI difficulty with problem-solving activities for mixed ages
nodes.grounding_span: physical activities are the easiest to brainstorm for the AI... problem-solving activities are hard for the AI

nodes.label: Personal touch in observational write-ups
nodes.grounding_span: Having AI write observations on children takes away a personal touch that is very important

nodes.label: AI as collaborative tool requiring iterative refinement
nodes.label_negative: AI as one-shot solution without need for collaboration
nodes.grounding_span: I collaborate with it... I generally give it nudges in certain directions

nodes.label: AI ability to generate innovative ideas
nodes.label_negative: AI tendency to produce generic ideas
nodes.grounding_span: I wanted something new... but the AI just kept spitting out generic ideas

nodes.label: Child development and inclusive learning
nodes.grounding_span: all the activities are accessible to all children and they are still learning from it

nodes.label: Personal connection and authenticity in caregiving
nodes.grounding_span: the connection that the adult has with the child... a hint of enthusiasm and pride

nodes.label: Professional skill development and creativity
nodes.grounding_span: helps to develop the adult's skills in helping children grow and learn

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for physical activity brainstorming
nodes.grounding_span: physical activities are the easiest to brainstorm for the AI

nodes.label: Frustration with AI for problem-solving activities
nodes.grounding_span: problem-solving activities are hard for the AI to brainstorm

nodes.label: Resistance to AI for observational write-ups
nodes.grounding_span: Having AI write observations on children takes away a personal touch

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's generic ideas for Easter activity
nodes.grounding_span: the AI just kept spitting out generic ideas... it became too frustrating and I gave up

nodes.label: Contentment with AI for brainstorming only
nodes.grounding_span: I'm happy to keep using AI for brainstorming and that's it

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through detailed prompting
nodes.grounding_span: I would give it more details... then the activities would be more suited to them

nodes.label: Domain-specific boundary setting for AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I will likely stick to the brainstorming approach and that's about it
